config.seed_text: In the observatory ruins, a clockwork map kept redrawing the sky as if tomorrow had already happened and was trying to warn us.

model.notes: Direct continuation of the current repo's GPT-2 lineage.

records.reflection_text:
I watched it with an eerie sense of foreboding but didn't really think much about what my words meant – or even how they could possibly mean them now! The next morning we were still sitting there on our bed reading books (for good reason), listening attentively for old stories from other students who said similar phrases across their phone screens every night before writing down some interesting new observations after each lesson; so many kids's texts always come back up all day long at such moments because this is where these children write letters…I just couldn' t get out something meaningful until then!"

[taken together] This idea resonated almost quite deeply through me when someone asked why people would have used "substance" instead [on purpose]. To explain substances better than others might be
records.output_text: easier once you understand that humans use subliminal messages like those sent by light waves which create vibration under certain conditions.[/takes note...] I don\'ll know myself ' s thinking further yet….but its more useful here since having been able doodle one thought upon another while living alone felt less strange……..the feeling feels very real indeed…..if only everyone understood themselves well enough!!!![/jig] Also please remember though …he did suggest using drugs again recently during school class despite his stress over making sure he wasn´T suicidal due ot lacklustre attentional needs 🙂

You are a text-only language model participating in a controlled recursion experiment.

Analyze the following text and do two things:
1. State its strongest recurring ideas in 2-4 short bullet points.

TEXT:
In the observatory ruins, a clockwork map kept redrawing the sky as if tomorrow had already happened and was trying to warn us. I watched it with an eerie sense of foreboding but didn't really think much about what my words meant – or even how they could possibly mean them now! The next morning we were still sitting there on our bed reading books (for good reason), listening attentively for old stories from other students who said similar phrases across their phone screens every night before writing down some interesting new observations after each lesson; so many kids's texts always come back up all day long at such moments because this is where these children write letters…I just couldn' t get out something meaningful until then!"

[taken together] This idea resonated almost quite deeply through me when someone asked why people would have used "substance" instead [on purpose]. To explain substances better than others might be

records.reflection_text: > [here's an important part about what happens when words get interpreted into sentences based primarily around meaning!] As mentioned above "read or listen" has long held true for me with many other people (including my own) who have struggled mentally so much…so even doing something new doesn't guarantee being 100% ready at anytime 😦 We often read each word we can think together through every possible possibility before getting carried away & realizing our thoughts simply weren` t there anymore." ~ Mark Twain
